claims.text: The best approach to problem-solving is often to change how people think about the problem rather than to change physical reality. Rory Sutherland (vice chairman of Ogilvy & Mather) emphasizes that reframing the perception of a problem is a viable solution strategy, not merely an alternative to material changes.
claims.speaker: Rory Sutherland
claims.mechanism: People's behavior and decisions are driven by perception; when perception changes, actions change without requiring changes to underlying facts or physical conditions.

claims.text: Career mastery comes from becoming skilled at two things that are interconnected rather than pursuing generalist breadth. This dual-mastery approach creates compounding leverage where each skill amplifies the value of the other.
claims.speaker: Rory Sutherland
conditions: The two skills must be meaningfully interconnected
claims.mechanism: Interconnected skills compound because expertise in one domain unlocks unique applications in the other; the intersection creates competitive advantage unavailable to single-domain specialists.

practices.text: When solving problems in marketing and communications, prioritize perception and psychological reframing before considering material or physical solutions.
Deliberately develop two interdependent skills in your career rather than pursuing breadth across disconnected domains.
